protocol.name: wlr_layer_shell_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: zwlr_layer_shell_v1

description.summary: create surfaces that are layers of the desktop

interface.description: Clients can use this interface to assign the surface_layer role to wl_surfaces. Such surfaces are assigned to a "layer" of the output and are rendered with a defined z-depth respective to each other. They may also be anchored to the edges and corners of a screen and specify input handling semantics. This interface should be suitable for the implementation of many desktop shell components, and a number of flags are provided to assist applications with data such as struts, margins, and anchors.

request.name: get_layer_surface

description.summary: create a layer_surface from a surface

request.description:
Create a layer surface for an existing surface. This assigns the role of layer_surface, or raises a protocol error if another role is already assigned.
Creating a layer surface from a wl_surface which has a buffer attached or committed is a client error, and any attempts by a client to attach or manipulate a buffer prior to the first layer_surface.configure call must also be treated as errors.
You may pass NULL for output to allow the compositor to decide which output to use. Generally this will be the one that the user most recently interacted with.
Clients can specify a namespace that defines the purpose of the layer surface.

arg.name: layer
arg.summary: layer to add this surface to

entry.name: already_constructed
entry.summary: wl_surface has a buffer attached or committed

request.name: destroy
request.description: This request indicates that the client will not use the layer_shell object any more. Objects that have been created through this instance are not affected.

interface.name: zwlr_layer_surface_v1
interface.description:
An interface that may be implemented by a wl_surface, for surfaces that are designed to be rendered as a layer of a stacked desktop-like environment.
Layer surface state (layer, size, anchor, exclusive zone, keyboard interactivity) is double-buffered, and will be applied at the time wl_surface.commit of the corresponding wl_surface is called.
Attaching a null buffer to a layer surface unmaps it.
Unmapping a layer_surface of the currently focused window will result in the layer_surface losing focus. The compositor should then focus another surface.
The client must set an anchor of at least one, or set the size to a non-zero width and height, otherwise the compositor will raise a bad_anchor error.

request.name: set_size

description.summary: sets the size of the surface

request.description:
Sets the size of the surface in surface-local coordinates. The compositor will display the surface with the given size and may ignore its buffer contents; the compositor may choose to force the surface to be larger or smaller than the requested size.
If you pass 0 for either value, the compositor will assign it and inform you of the assignment in the configure event. You must set your anchor to opposite edges in the axis the compositor is determining the size of and not include a margin in the axis the compositor is determining the size of, or else the request will fail and the compositor will raise a protocol error.
The width and height arguments are in surface-local coordinates.

request.name: set_anchor

description.summary: configures the anchor point of the surface

request.description:
Requests that the compositor anchor the surface to the specified edges and corners. If two opposite edges are specified, the compositor will spread the surface in between.
On compositors implementing xdg-output, if two opposite edges of the compositor's output are specified, the compositor will cover the full surface of the output's work area.
The edges and corners are described by the anchor enum. All anchor values are valid.

request.name: set_exclusive_zone

description.summary: configures the exclusive geometry of this surface

request.description:
Requests that the compositor avoids occluding an area with other surfaces. The compositor's use of this information is implementation-dependent - do not assume that this region will not actually be occluded.
A positive value is only meaningful if the surface is anchored to one edge or an edge and both perpendicular edges. If the surface is not anchored, anchored to more than one edge (not including perpendicular edges), or anchored to a perpendicular edge, the exclusive_zone is ignored.
The exclusive_zone is calculated from the edge that the surface is anchored to. If the surface is anchored to the top edge, the exclusive_zone is the distance from the top edge to the top of the surface. If the surface is anchored to the bottom edge, the exclusive_zone is the distance from the bottom of the surface to the bottom edge.
Setting the exclusive_zone to -1 indicates that the layer surface does not wish to move or resize to accommodate for other surfaces, and hints that the exclusive_zone should not be taken into account for the surface. This is useful when the surface wants to take the full space of the screen.
If the compositor respects requests for arbitrary values of the exclusive_zone, the compositor will place new layer surfaces respecting the most recently requested exclusive_zone.

request.name: set_margin

description.summary: sets a margin from the anchor point

request.description:
Requests that the surface be placed some distance away from the anchor point on the output, in surface-local coordinates. Setting this value for edges you are not anchored to has no effect.
The exclusive_zone includes the margin for the relevant edge.

request.name: set_keyboard_interactivity
request.description:
Set how keyboard events are delivered to this surface. By default, layer shell surfaces do not receive keyboard events; this request can be used to enable delivery of keyboard events to a surface.
Keyboard events from existing or new input devices will be delivered normally. Other clients will not receive keyboard events while this surface has focus, as per normal Wayland semantics.
Advisory: it is discouraged to set this value to anything other than none for desktop backgrounds, dock or status bars, notification feeds, and similar layer shell use cases. These surfaces usually do not need to receive keyboard input and should not be stealing it from other clients.
This setting is inherited by child surfaces set by the get_popup request.
Layer surfaces receive pointer, touch, and tablet events normally. If you do not want to receive them, set the input region on your surface to an empty region.
Clients that do not set the interactivity to none may be moved by the compositor above the keyboard focus surface. This is compositor- specific behavior.

request.name: get_popup

description.summary: assign this layer_surface as an xdg_popup parent

request.description:
This assigns an xdg_popup's parent to this layer_surface. This popup should have been created via xdg_surface::get_popup with the parent set to NULL, and this request must be invoked before committing the popup's initial state.
See the documentation of xdg_popup for more details about what an xdg_popup is and how it is used.

request.name: ack_configure
request.description:
When a configure event is received, if a client commits the surface in response to the configure event, then the client must make an ack_configure request sometime before the commit request, passing along the serial of the configure event.
If the client receives multiple configure events before it can respond to one, it only has to ack the last configure event.
A client is not required to commit immediately after sending an ack_configure request - it may even ack_configure several times before its next surface commit.
A client may send multiple ack_configure requests before committing, but only the last request sent before a commit indicates which configure event the client really is responding to.

arg.name: serial
arg.summary: the serial from the configure event

request.name: destroy
request.description: This request destroys the layer surface.

event.name: configure
event.description:
The configure event asks the client to resize its surface.
Clients should arrange their surface for the new states, and then send an ack_configure request with the serial sent in this configure event at some point before committing the new surface.
The client is free to dismiss all but the last configure event it received.
The width and height arguments specify the size of the window in surface-local coordinates.
The size is a hint, in the sense that the client is free to ignore it if it doesn't resize, pick a smaller size (to satisfy aspect ratio or resize in steps of NxM pixels). If the client picks a smaller size and is anchored to two opposite edges (i.e. is in a mode that would normally result in the surface being bigger than the hint from the configure event), the compositor can place the layer surface anywhere it likes. In addition, vertical anchors imply horizontal stretching (after accounting for margins), and horizontal anchors imply vertical stretching. Only when combined do both axes get stretched.
The compositor can send configure events at any time, even before the first attach. The surface is initially unmapped until the first ack_configure is sent and a buffer is attached.
The width and height are in surface local coordinates and are never zero at the same time.

event.name: closed
event.description: The closed event is sent by the compositor when the surface will no longer be shown. The output may have been destroyed or the user may have asked for it to be removed. Further changes to the surface will be ignored. The client should destroy the resource after receiving this event, and create a new surface if they so choose.

entry.name: none
entry.description:
This value indicates that this surface is not interested in keyboard events and the compositor should never assign it the keyboard focus.
This is the default value, set for newly created layer shell surfaces.
This is useful for e.g. desktop widgets that display information or only have interaction mechanisms that are not the keyboard.

entry.name: exclusive
entry.description:
Request exclusive keyboard focus if this surface is above the shell surface layer.
For the top and overlay layers, the seat will always give exclusive keyboard focus to the top-most layer which has keyboard interactivity set to exclusive. If this layer contains multiple surfaces with keyboard interactivity set to exclusive, the compositor determines the one receiving the keyboard focus in an implementation-defined manner. In this case, no guarantee is made when this surface will receive keyboard focus, if ever.
For the bottom and background layers, the compositor is allowed to use normal focus semantics.
This setting is mainly intended for applications that need to ensure they receive all keyboard events, such as a lock screen or a password prompt.

entry.name: on_demand
entry.description: This requests the compositor to allow this surface to be focused and unfocused by the user in an implementation-defined manner. The user must be able to unfocus this surface.

entry.name: top
entry.summary: the top edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: bottom
entry.summary: the bottom edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: left
entry.summary: the left edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: right
entry.summary: the right edge of the anchor rectangle
